title: pinned hash builtin
manager: A single blessed hash function pinned to an exact algorithm (named in the conformance vectors), so a machine that hashes agrees with every other implementation forever. This is contract-coupled infrastructure — the same pinning discipline as the canonical source hash — because a hash that varies by host is worse than no hash at all: it looks like it works right up until two systems compute different digests for the same bytes.
cs: pinned hash builtin, algorithm named in the vectors; over bytearray/string; contract-coupled (sibling of C1, W6.99); cross-host identical.
